title: Pale Orbit
desc: The floating light chamber needs its reflectors and shade shutters reset before the deeper tunnels vanish into guesswork.

steps.text:
The deeper chambers of the Catacombs are not dark so much as selectively visible. Pale blue orbs drift under the ceiling in slow measured paths, bright enough to pull the carvings out of the stone and soft enough to leave the far alcoves mysterious on purpose. Ozone hangs in the air beside a faint floral scent that seems impossible this far below the desert. When the light circuit is balanced, the whole chamber looks less excavated than suspended, like the tunnels were waiting for the orbs to decide what mattered before they agreed to exist.

The light keeper stands beside a row of shallow reflector basins with her sleeves rolled to the elbow. "Polish the stone basins if you want the lower work," she says. "Or reset the vent shutters above if you would rather climb. The orbs drift fine on their own. It is the chamber around them that keeps forgetting how to receive them."

options.label: Polish the reflector basins along the chamber floor
Reset the vent shutters above the light path

steps.text:
The basins are cut from pale stone and filled with a film of clear oil that turns each drifting orb into a second floating light below your feet. Dust and mineral haze have clouded half of them into dull circles. You wipe the stone rims clean, skim the surface oil, and restore the polished interior until the orbs begin appearing in them again one by one. As the reflections return, the chamber doubles in quiet beauty. Blue light moves above you and below you, the carvings on the pillars catching both passes at once.

By the time you finish the last basin, the far tunnel mouth no longer looks abandoned. It looks invited. The keeper studies the restored line and nods. "Better. If a place is going to disturb people, it should at least do so elegantly."

options.label: Cap the oil jar and leave the chamber glowing in two directions

steps.text:
The upper vent shutters do more than move air. They decide how much of the chamber's light is allowed to flatten under the vents and how much stays gathered in the center where the Conjurors prefer it. Two have drifted wide, washing one wall pale and leaving the rear arch deeper in shadow than the keeper will tolerate. You climb the side ladder, loosen the catches, and tip each shutter back to the alignment marks until the chamber's light regains its slow even balance.

The change is subtle until you stop moving. Then the whole room seems to exhale. Blue light collects where it should. The floral scent settles instead of streaking toward the vents. Even the distant hum from the lower tunnels feels more symmetrical. The keeper glances up through the restored line and allows herself a small approving tilt of the head. "Good. Now the catacombs may resume being unsettling for reasons other than poor maintenance."

options.label: Latch the shutters and climb down into the blue light
